sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.65 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: A finite simple group is called a$K_n$-group\/ if its order is divisible by exactly$n$different primes. The number of$K_3$-groups is known to be~8. The$K_4$-groups are classified mod CFSG (W.\,J.\,Shi, in: Group Theory in China$($Math. Appl., 365), Kluwer, 1996, 163--181) and some significant further results are obtained in (Yann Bugeaud, Zhenfu Cao, M.\,Mignotte, J.~Algebra, 241 (2001), 658--668)...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
